endpoints.purpose: White-label branding + feature flags for the mobile app (pre-login theming)
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.6.0 mobile API, extended 1.7.1 for anonymous-posting + attachments app parity + terms/privacy EULA screen (app-config-controller delta 2026-07-13). Public read (matches /push/vapid-key). Returns app_name (Community Title, falls back to site name), accent_color, logo_url, login_bg_url, terms_url/privacy_url (Settings -> General -> Community Setup; privacy_url falls back to get_privacy_policy_url() when unset; always strings, '' if not configured), pro_active, app_enabled (bool, default false in free -- b46c84f Pro-only mobile-app gate; jetonomy-pro flips it true via jetonomy_app_config only with a valid license, app refuses sign-in otherwise, fail closed), features{messaging,reactions,polls,badges,custom_fields,web_push,native_push,anonymous,attachments,blocking} (anonymous/attachments are ordinary Pro extension IDs; blocking is a FREE capability hardcoded true, no admin toggle -- BlockedUser model + /users/me/blocks), attachments{enabled,max_files,max_size_bytes,allowed_types,allowed_mimes} object (always emitted, enabled:false + zeroed limits when off, sourced from the SAME Extensions\Attachments\Settings::get() the web composer reads -- Pro listener in class-jetonomy-pro.php::gate_app_on_license()/attachments_config()). When the Pro license is invalid, gate_app_on_license() forces every Pro-derived features.* flag to false (blocking excluded) as belt-and-braces consistency. Wrapped in the jetonomy_app_config filter.

endpoints.purpose: Global cross-space home feed (sort=hot|new|top), visibility-gated, paginated
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.6.0 mobile API. Feed_Controller extends Posts_Controller so the post shape (incl. is_bookmarked + viewer_vote) is shared. Gating in SQL via Post::list_global_feed (member-or-public spaces, is_private=0). Standard paginated_response envelope + X-WP-Total / X-WP-TotalPages headers.

endpoints.purpose: List all spaces or create new space
endpoints.audit_notes: GET: visibility-filtered; POST: create_permission_check enforces jetonomy_create_spaces. 1.4.1 A11: GET wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode. 1.6.0: each space in the GET response carries viewer-relative is_member, viewer_role, is_subscribed (null-safe for logged-out).

endpoints.purpose: Get, update, or delete a space
endpoints.audit_notes: GET: membership for private/hidden; PATCH/DELETE: space-admin check. 1.4.1 A11: GET wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode. 1.6.0: GET response adds is_member, viewer_role, is_subscribed.

endpoints.purpose: List space members or join space
endpoints.audit_notes: GET: membership for private/hidden; POST: join_policy enforced. 1.4.1 A11: GET wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.
Public read so anonymous visitors see the same 'Managed by' sidebar as members. 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.

endpoints.purpose: List pending join requests for a space (space moderators/admins)
endpoints.audit_notes: Front-end twin of the wp-admin Join Requests tab; is_space_privileged checked inside the handler.

endpoints.purpose: Approve a pending join request and admit the member
endpoints.audit_notes: Reuses JoinRequest::approve + SpaceMember::add; fires jetonomy_join_request_approved (Notifier in-app + email).
Reuses JoinRequest::deny; fires jetonomy_join_request_denied (Notifier in-app + email).

endpoints.purpose: Update member role or leave space
endpoints.audit_notes: PATCH/DELETE: space-admin check
Generate invite link

1.8.0: added for the front-end members-page invite panel. Route permission is the community-visibility gate; the space-admin check is in the callback (mirrors get_join_requests). Space ADMIN not privileged - a token is a bearer credential into a possibly-hidden space, so listing is disclosing.

1.8.0: added for the front-end members-page invite panel. InviteLink::revoke() scopes the DELETE to id AND space_id, so another space's link cannot be revoked through this route; a false return is a 404.

endpoints.purpose: Accept invite link and join space
endpoints.audit_notes: Accept invite link. 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.

endpoints.purpose: List posts in space or create new post
endpoints.audit_notes: GET: space-read enforced; POST: create_posts enforced. 1.4.1 A11: GET wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode. 1.6.0: each post in the GET response carries is_bookmarked + viewer_vote (-1|0|1), batch-resolved via enrich_viewer_state (no N+1); null-safe for logged-out.

endpoints.purpose: List draft posts for current user
endpoints.audit_notes: List user's drafts

endpoints.purpose: Get, update, or delete a single post
endpoints.audit_notes: GET: can_read_post; PATCH/DELETE: ownership or edit_others. 1.4.1 A11: GET wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode. 1.6.0: GET response adds is_bookmarked + viewer_vote (-1|0|1). 1.8.0: can_read_post now also gates STATUS - a non-publish post (trash/pending/spam) is readable only by its author or a moderator, everyone else gets 403; delete is a soft delete so this is what makes DELETE /posts/{id} actually stop serving the content. 1.8.0: blocked_author is ADVISORY ONLY - title/content/content_plain now come back empty server-side (Post::apply_block_tombstone) when it is true, so a client that ignores the flag still cannot render the blocked author's words.
Close/lock post

endpoints.purpose: Pin post to top of space
endpoints.audit_notes: Pin to top of space
Move to another space
Merge with another post
POST creates/toggles a vote; DELETE removes it

endpoints.purpose: Set the roadmap status (planned, in_progress, shipped, declined) on an Ideas-type post. Fires jetonomy_idea_status_changed.
endpoints.audit_notes: Added 1.4.2 with the Ideas roadmap. Outer gate is REST_Auth::auth_mutation (closure); inner gate is check_permission('close_posts', $space_id) which requires space trust level 3 (moderator). Validates via Post::valid_idea_statuses() so adding a new lane requires no controller change. Returns 400 if the post's space type is not 'ideas'.

endpoints.purpose: Fetch OG metadata for link preview
endpoints.audit_notes: Fetch OG metadata. 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.

endpoints.purpose: List replies or create new reply
endpoints.audit_notes: GET: can_read_post on the PARENT post; POST: create_replies. 1.4.1 A11: GET wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode. 1.8.0: GET was space-read only, which left the whole thread under a trashed/pending/private post readable; it now gates on the parent post so deleting a topic takes its conversation with it.
1.6.0 drift fix: no GET is registered on this route (only PATCH + DELETE) - the stale GET/get_item entry was removed.

endpoints.purpose: Mark / unmark a reply as the accepted answer
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.6.0 drift fix: DELETE -> unaccept_reply was missing; handler is accept_reply (POST) + unaccept_reply (DELETE), not accept_answer.
1.6.0 drift fix: handler method is split_reply (was wrongly listed as split_item).
POST creates/toggles a vote; DELETE removes it
Get current user

endpoints.purpose: Update own profile (display_name, bio, avatar_url, settings, notification_preferences)
endpoints.audit_notes: Was missing from the manifest (delta added 1.5.0). avatar_url is consumed at render since 1.5.0 by Jetonomy\Avatar (pre_get_avatar_data filter, Gravatar fallback) - local avatar upload flows POST /media then PATCH here (#9966775705).

endpoints.purpose: In-app account deletion (Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) / GDPR Art. 17). Body: { password?, confirm: 'DELETE', delete_content?: bool }. Default content policy is anonymize (author_id -> 0 tombstone via the existing Privacy::on_user_delete() contract); delete_content=true opt-in hard-deletes authored posts/replies via internal rest_do_request() calls to DELETE /posts/{id} and DELETE /replies/{id} so counters + the Pro attachments cascade (jetonomy_after_delete_post/reply) stay correct. Rate-limited 5/hour/IP via the (now-shared) Base_Controller::check_rate_limit('delete_account'). Refuses manage_options users (403 jetonomy_admin_must_use_wp_admin). Multisite: defaults to remove_user_from_blog() for the current site only; wpmu_delete_user() (network-wide) only when jetonomy_delete_account_network_wide filter returns true.
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.7.1. Attachments preserved by default (post_types_to_delete_with_user filtered to drop 'attachment', then reassigned to author_id=0) unless delete_content=true. Tombstoned (author_id=0) content now renders '[deleted]' via Author::for_display() - see hooks_fired delta.
Get user profile (public). 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode. Note: PATCH for users is on /users/me only, not the numeric-ID route.
Get user by login. 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.
Get user's posts. 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.
1.6.0 drift fix: permission callback is `fn() => is_user_logged_in()` (mention autocomplete is login-only), not __return_true/public.
List user's notifications 1.4.3: gained ?filter= query arg (all|unread|mentions|replies|system).
Get unread count
Mark all as read
Mark single notification
List/create subscriptions
Delete subscription

endpoints.purpose: Full-text search posts, replies, spaces, users
endpoints.audit_notes: Full-text search. 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode. 1.7.1: gained real limit/offset pagination (limit 1-50, default 20; per_page accepted as an alias for limit, matching assets/js/header.js) - the route previously declared no pagination args at all and every search_* query used a hardcoded LIMIT 20/10. X-WP-Total + X-WP-TotalPages now set; the hand-rolled meta.has_more (always true past page 1) replaced with the correct offset-based paginated_response() calculation. type=all mode's meta gained totals{posts,spaces,tags} - meta.total now means the posts group's real total, not the combined row count of all three groups.
GET: public; POST: manage_categories cap. 1.4.1 A11: GET wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.
GET: public; PATCH/DELETE: manage_categories. 1.4.1 A11: GET wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.
List tags (only GET registered - no POST/PATCH/DELETE in current code). 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.
Get leaderboard. 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.
Both methods gated by is_user_logged_in() inline closure (A3 cleanup).
Delete bookmark

endpoints.purpose: List the current user's blocked users, or block a user (body: {user_id:int})
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.7.1 foundation (step 1 of 2, App Store Guideline 1.2 safety primitive). GET mirrors the bookmarks-controller is_user_logged_in() pattern; POST delegates to BlockedUser::block() which guards self-block (400), nonexistent target (404), and blocking a manage_options/jetonomy_moderate user (403). List items are batch-enriched via Base_Controller::batch_load_users() (no N+1). Read-surface filtering (feed/search/notifications/etc.) is a separate follow-up — this model is inert until those call sites adopt BlockedUser::exclusion_sql().
1.7.1 foundation. Mirrors Bookmarks_Controller::delete_item — 200 with {deleted:true,user_id} even when no block existed (idempotent no-op).

endpoints.purpose: List flagged content in moderation queue
endpoints.audit_notes: List moderation queue
Approve flagged content
Mark as spam
Move to trash

endpoints.purpose: List flags or create new flag
endpoints.audit_notes: GET: list flags (mod-only); POST: flag (jetonomy_flag enforced)
List flags for moderation
Resolve flag

endpoints.purpose: Bulk approve/spam/trash content (REST parity for wp_ajax_jetonomy_bulk_content_action)
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.4.1 A4: REST parity with the wp-admin AJAX bulk handler. Per-item helpers (approve_item/spam_item/trash_item) are extracted so single + bulk paths fire the same `jetonomy_content_moderated` action and reputation penalty. Per-item failures surfaced in the row's `status` field rather than aborting the batch.

endpoints.purpose: List all flags filed against a single post
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.4.1 A5: mod-only inspection view backed by `Flag::find_for_post()`. Returns `[]` (200) - never 404 - when the post has no flags. Cap-gated (anon=403) in private mode rather than getting the visibility 401 flip.
List/create bans
Unban user
List flags in space
Resolve flag in space
Moderate content in space

endpoints.permission: Visibility::rest_check + handler: post-scope follows Permission_Engine::can_read_post (anon OK on readable posts); global/space scopes require_auth
endpoints.audit_notes: Activity updates. 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.
oEmbed endpoint. 1.4.1 A11: wrapped with Visibility::rest_check - responds 401 in private mode.

Upload media. Capability matrix enforced in the permission_callback (was auth_mutation('read') — any logged-in user; F13); upload_image() additionally re-checks Restriction::is_banned/is_silenced and returns 403.
Login endpoint (rate-limited). 1.5.0: accepts optional captcha_token, verified via Captcha_Manager::verify_or_skip(0, ...) - parity with register/lost-password (#9977126420).
Register endpoint (rate-limited, anti-spam)
Password reset (rate-limited, enumeration-proof)
Email verification token

endpoints.purpose: Fresh wp_rest nonce for the calling session (no-cache headers)
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.5.0 - backs jetonomy-rest.js 403 retry (rest_cookie_invalid_nonce). Re-validates the logged_in cookie before minting because core downgrades nonce-less REST requests to user 0; same trust basis as core admin-ajax rest-nonce.
Resend verification (rate-limited, enumeration-proof)
Rebuild counters
Update trust level

endpoints.purpose: Delete a single notification (own user only)
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.4.3 feature: per-row delete from the notifications inbox. Handler scopes the delete to current_user_id() so users can never delete another user's notifications.

endpoints.purpose: Bulk mark-as-read or delete a batch of notifications
endpoints.audit_notes: 1.4.3 feature: bulk toolbar in the notifications admin template. Accepts {ids: int[], action: "mark_read"|"delete"}. Handler scopes both operations to current_user_id().

ajax.purpose: Reset a single email-template row to its shipped default (1.4.1 A8)

admin_pages.title: Activity Log
admin_pages.source: includes/admin/class-admin.php (1.4.1 A6: read-only audit browser over jt_activity_log; list table at includes/admin/class-activity-list-table.php; view at includes/admin/views/activity.php)

admin_pages.title: Revisions
admin_pages.source: includes/admin/class-admin.php (1.4.1 A7: read-only revisions browser with per-object diff view; list table at includes/admin/class-revisions-list-table.php; view at includes/admin/views/revisions.php; backed by Revision::list_objects_with_revisions() + count_objects_with_revisions())

settings.controls: base_slug, front_page, accent_color, text_color, bg_color, bg_subtle_color, border_color, default_space_type, notification_defaults, trust_thresholds, rate_limits, activity_log_retention_days, require_email_verification, frontend_space_creation_roles, reputation_points, terms_url, privacy_url
settings.notes: 1.4.3: gained reputation_points array (action_slug → int) for the new configurable-reputation settings card; sanitize_settings filters to known action slugs from Reputation::action_points_defaults(). 1.5.0: gained front_page (bool, default false) - "Community as Homepage"; 1.8.0: that request-filter injection (Router::maybe_serve_front_page) is GONE. It made the front page's query vars non-empty, which broke WP's own is_front_page() resolution and left the real page unqueryable — so the owner's per-page SEO on it (e.g. their Yoast title) was unreachable. Router::handle_request() now derives the home route at template_redirect via is_mapped_front_page(), leaving WP's resolution intact, and Template_Loader::set_seo_meta() defers entirely on that mapped page because a real page owns its own SEO. 1.5.0: gained the Color Palette set (text_color, bg_color, bg_subtle_color, border_color; empty = no override) - Template_Loader::palette_tokens()/palette_css() emit root token overrides whenever a palette field is set (accent_color treats its #0073aa default as a "not set" sentinel, which is what makes theme adoption the default); Theme_Integration light bridge subtracts palette-set tokens so explicit admin picks beat auto theme bridging. 1.7.1: gained terms_url + privacy_url (Settings -> General -> Community Setup) - consumed by GET /app/config for the mobile app's pre-login EULA screen; privacy_url falls back to get_privacy_policy_url() when unset. 1.8.0: inherit_fonts + inherit_colors REMOVED — both option keys and both checkboxes. inherit_fonts did nothing: measured byte-identical across 11 themes, because the --jt-font chain already ends in `inherit`, so the token resolves empty and .jt-app inherits the theme font either way — a checkbox governing a font setting that does not exist. inherit_colors was worse than dead: checked by default, it made palette_tokens() return empty, so an owner who picked an accent had it silently discarded, and the first Save destroyed a colour that had been applying (the key was absent, so the runtime had read it as unchecked). Theme adoption is now unconditional via the chains in jetonomy.css; the accent field is the single override.

settings.controls: user_welcome, reply_to_post, reply_to_reply, mention, accepted_answer, new_post_in_sub, badge_earned, vote_on_post, moderation, join_request, verification_reminder
settings.notes: 1.4.1 A8: `verification_reminder` row added to the editor allowlist + view + sample fixtures; defaults centralized via `Notifier::get_default_template()`; per-row Reset-to-default button gated by `wp_ajax_jetonomy_email_reset` (cap jetonomy_manage_settings, nonce jetonomy_admin).

settings.controls: Broadcast Jetonomy topics to the paired BuddyPress group activity stream. '0' disables.
settings.notes: 1.5.0: surfaced in Settings > Integrations (tab shown only when BuddyPress Groups active); own settings group so other tabs' saves never reset it. Read by Integrations\BuddyPress::broadcast_enabled().

settings.controls: Mirror BuddyPress activity comments on broadcast items back as Jetonomy replies. '0' disables.
settings.notes: 1.5.0: surfaced in Settings > Integrations. Read by Integrations\BuddyPress::comment_bridge_enabled().

migrations.change: ALTER ENUM idea_status to 4 documented lanes; remap legacy data
migrations.purpose: Aligns code with the 1.4.2 readme contract (planned, in_progress, shipped, declined). Earlier 1.4.2.1 backfill set rows to 'submitted' / 'completed' which the 4-lane UI does not render.
Rate-limit for the new jetonomy_verification_reminder cron - populated once per nudge so each unverified user receives at most one reminder.

tables.name: {prefix}jt_blocked_users
tables.purpose: User-level blocks (Apple Guideline 1.2 safety primitive, 1.7.1). Composite PK (blocker_id, blocked_id) is the uniqueness constraint; KEY blocker_created backs list_by_blocker(), KEY blocked_id backs is_blocked_between(). One-way semantics: only the blocker stops seeing the blocked user's content. Foundation step (1 of 2) — no read-surface filtering wired yet.

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_delete_account_network_wide
hooks_fired.notes: 1.7.1. DELETE /users/me multisite branch. Default false = remove_user_from_blog() for the current site only (a shared network account is not this site's to fully delete). Return true to route through wpmu_delete_user() instead (network-wide) for a network that owns exactly one community.

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_app_config
hooks_fired.notes: 1.6.0. Filters the GET /app/config payload (branding + feature flags) so Pro / site owners can override white-label branding or force-flag a feature.

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_after_create_post
hooks_fired.where: includes/api/class-posts-controller.php (REST create); includes/models/class-post.php Post::publish_scheduled() (cron-published scheduled post, $request null); includes/class-abilities.php (programmatic create, $request null)

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_after_create_reply
hooks_fired.where: includes/api/class-replies-controller.php (REST create); includes/notifications/class-notifier.php on_reply_from_email() ($request null); includes/class-abilities.php (programmatic create, $request null)
hooks_fired.audit_notes: 1.7.1: widened 2 -> 3 args to mirror jetonomy_after_create_post. The REST create path (class-replies-controller.php) now passes $request so jetonomy-pro's attachments extension can read attachment_ids from a JSON body on POST /posts/{id}/replies (previously silently dropped for mobile/JSON clients, which never populate $_POST).

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_content_moderated
hooks_fired.where: includes/moderation/class-moderation-service.php apply_object_status() — single choke-point; REST mod controller, admin AJAX content handler, abilities, and space-mod all funnel through here. Canonical action vocabulary: approve|spam|trash|hold.

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_moderation_flag_resolution
hooks_fired.where: includes/moderation/class-moderation-service.php apply_object_status() — filters the status applied to an object's pending flags when content is moderated directly (default approve=dismissed, spam/trash=valid, hold=''). Return '' to leave flags pending.

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_trusted_proxies
hooks_fired.where: includes/functions.php Jetonomy\client_ip() — site owner declares trusted reverse-proxy / CDN IPs so X-Forwarded-For is honoured for IP-bans + rate limiting. Empty by default (XFF ignored; REMOTE_ADDR used).

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_client_ip
hooks_fired.where: includes/functions.php Jetonomy\client_ip() — final resolved client IP; override for proxy setups the default trusted-proxy logic doesn't cover.

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_allow_self_upvote
hooks_fired.where: includes/api/class-votes-controller.php — whether a member may upvote their own content (default true). Return false to block self-upvote for a stricter reputation economy. Self-downvote is always blocked.

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_notification_created
hooks_fired.args: notification_id, user_id, type, object_type, object_id, message, link
hooks_fired.audit_notes: 1.8.0: BOTH fire sites now go through Notifier::emit_notification_created(), which does NOT fire this action when the recipient has blocked the actor (Notifier::recipient_blocked_actor). Consumers mirror a notification OUT of the app, so the gate belongs above the hook, not in each listener - Pro's web-push subscribed to the raw hook and fired 12 lines ABOVE the block check that correctly suppressed the matching EMAIL, so the same event produced a suppressed email and a delivered push. The signature is unchanged (7 args) and carries no actor_id, so a listener could not have made this decision itself. The notification ROW is still written; only the outbound mirror is withheld, and unblocking restores history without re-sending.

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_email_headers
hooks_fired.consumed_by: jetonomy-pro (reply-by-email injects Reply-To using $object_type/$object_id to mint a post-scoped token)

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_rest_prepare_space
hooks_fired.audit_notes: 1.7.1: consumed by jetonomy-pro's anonymous-posting extension (Extension::inject_allow_anonymous()) to add allow_anonymous:bool to the space payload, so the mobile app can gate the 'Post anonymously' toggle on the same per-space opt-in Gate::space_allows() already enforces server-side on write.

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_post_publish_transition
hooks_fired.where: includes/models/class-post.php (Post::create when created publish; Post::update on publish transitions)

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_space_feed_posts
hooks_fired.notes: 1.5.0 - extension point for the space RSS feed item list (max 20, newest first).

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_reply_publish_transition
hooks_fired.where: includes/models/class-reply.php (Reply::create when created publish; Reply::update on publish transitions)

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_space_member_role_changed
hooks_fired.consumed_by: Uncanny Automator glue (Pro/companion, class_exists guard); no free consumer

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_before_set_role
hooks_fired.consumed_by: veto hook for programmatic role changes; symmetry with jetonomy_before_join_space

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_pro_first_reaction
hooks_fired.where: CONSUMED by Notifier::on_first_reaction (fired by Pro reactions on 0->1)

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_author_can_reveal
hooks_fired.consumed_by: jetonomy-pro anonymous-posting (Reveal) - true only for manage_options in explicit reveal context

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_user_blocked
hooks_fired.notes: 1.7.1 foundation. Fires after a successful block (including re-blocking an already-blocked user, since the write is INSERT IGNORE).

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_user_unblocked
hooks_fired.notes: 1.7.1 foundation. Fires after unblock() runs, regardless of whether a row was actually deleted (idempotent no-op safe).

hooks_fired.name: jetonomy_blocked_user_ids
hooks_fired.notes: 1.7.1 foundation. Lets Pro/3rd-party code widen the exclusion set (e.g. a future mute feature) without touching the model. Applied once per request (self::$memo), after the 5-minute Cache::remember layer.

cron.purpose: Evaluate and update user trust levels
Clean up expired restrictions and bans
Prune old activity log entries (configurable retention via jetonomy_settings.activity_log_retention_days, default 90; 0 disables)
Mark old unread notifications as read (30-day cutoff)
Publish posts whose published_at datetime has passed

Send single-shot nudge email to users who never verified after `jetonomy_settings.verification_reminder_hours` (default 24, 0 disables). Rate-limited via `jt_user_profiles.verification_reminder_sent_at` column (1.4.1 migration). Self-heals: also lazy-scheduled on init to upgrade installs without re-activate (1.4.1 A10).

coding_rules: REST-first: every mutation reachable via jetonomy/v1/*
Every release zip must come from bin/build-release.sh

notes.manifest_quality: Generated 2026-04-29 under the wp-plugin-onboard skill's coverage gate (≥95% per category vs ground-truth grep counts). REST 64/64, AJAX 43/43, admin pages 14/14, cron 6/6, hooks_fired 99/99 (61 actions + 43 filters - see discrepancies note for stale entries) - all 100%. Settings count is 2 logical groups (vs 7 register_setting field-level calls - different counting basis, both correct).
notes.consumed_by_pro: Hooks marked consumed_by: 'jetonomy-pro' are confirmed via grep across the Pro plugin. These are the integration surface - Pro will break if they change.
notes.discrepancies_2026_04_29: Stale manifest entries surfaced during the 1.4.1 refresh - recorded but NOT fixed in code (per BUILD-LOG): (1) hooks_fired entries `jetonomy_admin_footer_text`, `jetonomy_extensions_init`, `jetonomy_footer_text`, `jetonomy_header_logo`, `jetonomy_post_response`, `jetonomy_profile_response` are documented but no longer fired anywhere in the free codebase - likely renamed during the 1.3.x cycle to `jetonomy_rest_prepare_*`. (2) cron entry `jetonomy_sync_membership` was previously documented but is no longer scheduled in `Cron::schedule()` - replaced in this refresh by `jetonomy_verification_reminder`. (3) REST entries `/votes/(?P<id>\d+)` (DELETE) and `/tags/(?P<id>\d+)` (GET/PATCH/DELETE) were stale stubs - actual code uses POST+DELETE on `/posts/{id}/vote` and `/replies/{id}/vote`, and `/tags/{id}` was never implemented - both removed in this refresh.

changelog.summary: 1.4.3 feature drop: top-level Admin Bar quick-jump (Jetonomy\Admin_Bar), unified .jetonomy-empty-state primitive (jetonomy_admin_empty_state() global helper), Notifications admin page redesigned (filter tabs, bulk toolbar, per-row menu, pagination, ?filter= on GET, DELETE /notifications/{id}, POST /notifications/bulk), configurable reputation (Reputation::action_points_map/action_points_defaults, jetonomy_reputation_points_for filter, reputation_points settings array). New Pro-consumption hooks: jetonomy_post_list_results_for_space, jetonomy_post_card_after_badges. a11y: focus-visible drift fixed on editor body and feed actions. BuddyPress dark-mode propagation fix, composer Private toggle spacing, Banned Users confirm tone.

1.4.2.x patch cycle: schema migration aligns idea_status ENUM with the documented 4-lane spec (planned, in_progress, shipped, declined); login block stays light in dark mode; setup wizard offers all 4 default-space-type options; manifest gains the missing POST /posts/{id}/idea-status REST endpoint and jetonomy_user_left_space hooks_fired entry.

1.4.2 release: Show & Tell short-form feed space type, Ideas roadmap statuses (planned/in progress/shipped/declined), Q&A accepted-answer pinning with 'Answered' badge on space list, idea-status notifications across activity log + email digest + in-app inbox. Performance: jetonomy_cron_batch_size filter caps cleanup cron handlers at 500 rows/run by default (trust evaluation, expired restrictions, old notifications, scheduled posts). Multisite: activation now installs tables on every existing subsite AND every new subsite created later (wp_initialize_site hook). i18n: composer, login block, IA state, banned-member notice, header escape hint, prefix builder, admin import flow fully translatable. a11y: visible focus indicators everywhere, aria-labels on filter / bulk-action / select-all controls. Fixes: per-page setting now controls list length AND Load More click count; vote controls hide (not just disable) when admin disables voting on a space; native confirms swapped for in-product modals; import progress AJAX now requires the right capability not just a nonce; setup wizard skips redirect under WP-CLI / REST contexts; settings save confirmations persist until dismissed.

1.4.1 refresh: +2 REST endpoints (`POST /moderation/bulk`, `GET /posts/{id}/flags`), +2 admin pages (Activity Log, Revisions), +1 AJAX (`jetonomy_email_reset`), +1 cron (`jetonomy_verification_reminder`), +1 DB migration (`jt_user_profiles.verification_reminder_sent_at`), +1 helper class (`Jetonomy\Visibility`), +2 hooks_fired (`jetonomy_verification_reminder_sent`, `jetonomy_rest_prepare_notification`). A11 visibility wrap applied to 20 public-read REST endpoints (every endpoint listed above with `audit_notes` mentioning `Visibility::rest_check`). Email-templates allowlist gained `verification_reminder` row (A8). Plus: 4 stale manifest entries cleaned out (see discrepancies_2026_04_29).

refresh_2026_04_30: v2 schema sections added on 2026-04-30 refresh. All `static_analysis.*` arrays default to []; populated entries are confirmed findings.

dead_listeners: free's 6 candidates (jetonomy_cleanup_expired, jetonomy_cleanup_notifications, jetonomy_prune_activity, jetonomy_publish_scheduled, jetonomy_trust_evaluation, jetonomy_verification_reminder) are wp_schedule_event() cron hooks, not dead - fired by WP-Cron, not direct do_action.
grid_track_overflow_risks: 4 bare 1fr tracks in fixed-column grids. Risk rated low (current cell content fits). Fix on next CSS sweep - convert to minmax(0, 1fr) for content-overflow safety.

generated.delta_2026_07_17_reply_permalinks: Reply deep links (Basecamp 10104728183). NEW canonical producer Jetonomy\reply_permalink($space_slug,$post_slug,$reply_id,$page=null) — the ONE builder of a reply URL, emitting ?rpg=N#reply-{id}; every previous producer concatenated its own '#reply-'.$id anchor for a DOM id that did not exist and a page that usually was not rendered. Consumers migrated: notifications-controller (REST object_url), functions.php notification_deep_link, notifications.php, notifier get_reply_url (email parity), user-profile.php, class-schema-markup (JSON-LD acceptedAnswer), class-fluent-community. NEW Jetonomy\replies_per_page() — single source for the setting, previously read raw in 3 places (single-post.php, Reply::list_by_post, and the new link-builder) which could drift and mis-target every link. NEW Models\Reply::page_of()/top_level_ancestor()/DEFAULT_SORT — SQL sibling COUNT (no thread load); nested replies resolve to their top-level ancestor's page. get_threaded() ORDER BY gains 'id ASC' tiebreak so it cannot disagree with page_of() on equal created_at (bulk imports). NEW template partial var 'permalink_page' (reply-card) so the single-post view passes the page it already rendered — measured 60 queries -> 0 for a 30-reply page. NEW CSS token --jt-scroll-offset (deep-link landing offset under host-theme sticky headers). REST CONTRACT CHANGE: reply_to_post notifications are now stored/returned as object_type='reply', object_id={reply_id} (was 'post'/{post_id}) — the row now knows WHICH reply it is about, which no schema change was needed for ('reply' was already in the jt_notifications ENUM and already the reply_to_reply shape). App/API consumers reading object_id on reply_to_post now get a reply id; pro web-push deep_link_for() correspondingly emits {type:'reply'} (a route it already handles for reply_to_reply). Pre-1.8.0 rows keep object_type='post' and degrade to the bare topic URL (today's behaviour) — no migration, no backfill.
generated.delta_2026_07_05_wave_d: Settings-that-didn't-apply fixes (Wave D, free side). NEW shared gate Notifier::should_email($user,$type) — master kill-switch (jetonomy_email_opt_out) + per-user per-type + admin default, applied at the create_and_maybe_email choke point AND the mention path (was 2 dup blocks, neither read the kill-switch); users-controller $valid_types allowlist widened (idea_status_changed/moderation/join_request were silently dropped on save). NEW Jetonomy\seo_settings() defaults union so the admin SEO checkboxes (Default:On) and the frontend consumers agree — seo_sitemap/seo_noindex_profiles/seo_noindex_search were phantom-off on fresh installs (routes: class-sitemap, class-schema-markup, admin render). NOTE: this flips sitemap/noindex ON for existing installs — an intentional SEO behavior change matching the UI. Advanced-moderation hold/spam/flag now enforced on the EDIT paths (posts+replies controllers), not just block (edit-to-spam bypass). NEW free provider filter jetonomy_sitemap_exclude_space (spaces+posts sitemap providers) consumed by seo-pro. wp-stubs: relaxed WP_Sitemaps_Provider signature to match core (the typed stub caused a false boot-smoke fatal once the sitemap actually loaded).
generated.delta_2026_07_04_wave_b: Privacy sweep #2 (Wave B). free:mentions no longer notifies users who can't read the mentioned content: Mentions::notify() gained ?int $space_id + bool $is_private params and filters recipients ONCE, set-based, before the loop via the new SpaceMember::members_among($space_id,$user_ids) batch helper (private/hidden spaces -> members only; is_private posts -> author + space staff via roles_for_users). Public spaces skip filtering (no query). Callers updated: posts-controller, replies-controller, notifier. No per-recipient N+1. Verified: outsider not notified / member notified on the private-space post.
generated.delta_2026_07_04_wave_a: Scale fixes (Wave A of the 1.6.0 audit). JETONOMY_DB_VERSION 1.5.0->1.6.0 + migration 1_6_0 adds 3 covering indexes: status_created(status,created_at) on jt_posts+jt_replies, object_created(object_type,object_id,created_at) on jt_votes, trust_user(trust_level,user_id) on jt_user_profiles. New model methods Post/Reply::list_by_status()+count_by_status() (shared by REST moderation queue and the wp-admin queue — killed 2 raw-SQL dups). GET /spaces/{id}/members and GET /moderation/queue are now paginated via the base get_pagination()/paginated_response() (moderation has_more was hard-coded false; queue pending-flags moved to X-Jetonomy-Pending-Flags header). New async cron action jetonomy_trust_evaluation_batch: the trust sweep is now a keyset-cursor (jetonomy_trust_eval_cursor option) Action-Scheduler fan-out that covers the whole base instead of re-running the same first 500; the CLI trust-evaluate shares Cron::run_trust_batch(). Embeds::process() now caches wp_oembed_get() via Cache::remember (oembed:<md5> key, DAY_IN_SECONDS, empty-string negative sentinel). Verified: lint + REST audit + boot smoke + live migration + functional REST/EXPLAIN on jetonomy.local.
generated.delta_2026_07_04: Post-refresh deltas + security-audit fixes. 3996dc0: Cache::remember_object() coerces empty-string persistent-cache hits to null (Redis/Memcached TypeError fix); free SEO baseline defers to seo-pro on post + space family (dupe og-tag fix). b46c84f: /app/config gains app_enabled (Pro mobile gate, fail closed) + app_name. Security fixes (2026-07-04 expectation audit): closed 5 private/hidden-space content leaks (Space::list_visible visibility-param bypass; space-members.php + space-roadmap.php missing read gate; search.php advanced-filter branch missing Space::content_visibility_sql; Schema_Markup space CollectionPage + breadcrumb JSON-LD). jetonomy_email_headers widened to 5 args ($object_type,$object_id) so pro reply-by-email can mint a post-scoped Reply-To token (was hooking the wrong adapter-level filter and fataling).
generated.refresh_2026_06_28: 1.6.0 mobile-app refresh + plugin.version 1.4.3->1.6.0. Verified the mobile surface is captured: GET /app/config (App_Config_Controller; serves app_name from Community Title, accent_color, logo_url + feature_flags map) and GET /feed (Feed_Controller; sort hot|new|top, cursor after/before + offset + window_days) present; logo_url added to Settings -> Appearance. REST 73/73 exact vs register_rest_route grep. App-side: the login screen now applies the discovered site accent+logo pre-auth (no plugin surface change). Native push device registration lives in jetonomy-pro web-push (/push/register-device), not free.
generated.refresh_2026_06_11: Coverage-gated refresh after the full-audit fix series (audit/full-audit-2026-06-11.md). wppqa baseline: audit/wppqa-baseline-2026-06-11. All categories within gate vs ground-truth grep: rest 68/69 calls (multi-config registrations), ajax 40/40 (comment-text hits excluded), hooks_fired 142/140+dynamic, tables 20 (3 dead tables dropped in Migration_1_5_0), blocks 8/8, shortcodes 8/8 (stale jetonomy_recent_activity entry removed), cron 6 (Action Scheduler map in class-cron.php - wp_schedule_event grep finds 0 by design), wp_cli 14 (dynamic dispatcher loop), admin_pages 14/14.
generated.from: wp-plugin-onboard --refresh (1.4.3 feature-drop delta: admin bar + notifications redesign + configurable reputation + Pro extension hooks)
generated.refresh_note: Refreshed 2026-05-14 for the 1.4.3 feature drop (commits 16215a2, 6bfebca, e2a3cd0, b0fa218). NEW: Admin_Bar class (includes/class-admin-bar.php, admin_bar_menu priority 60). NEW: jetonomy_admin_empty_state() global helper (includes/helpers.php). NEW: 3 public hooks for Pro consumption (jetonomy_post_list_results_for_space, jetonomy_post_card_after_badges, jetonomy_reputation_points_for). NEW: 2 REST endpoints on Notifications_Controller (DELETE /notifications/{id}, POST /notifications/bulk) plus ?filter= on the existing GET. NEW: configurable reputation via Reputation::action_points_map() + filter + reputation_points settings array. Notifications admin template fully redesigned (filter tabs, bulk toolbar, per-row menu, pagination, empty-state primitive). See audit/wppqa-baseline-2026-05-14/SUMMARY.md for the surface-area delta.
generated.static_analysis_refresh_2026_06_03: 1.5.0-dev bug-sweep refresh. Phase-0 wppqa baseline saved to audit/wppqa-baseline-2026-06-03/SUMMARY.md (authoritative current bug state). grid_track_overflow_risks cleared: all 4 flagged selectors (.jt-stats-bar, .jt-badges, .jt-kanban, tablet) verified to already use repeat(N, minmax(0, 1fr)) in jetonomy.css:691/697/705/774 — the 617/623/631/694 line refs were 1.4.3-era stale. dead_listeners still 1 (jetonomy_after_resolve_flag — intentional extension point, not a bug). No new issues from the bug sweep; wppqa errors are heuristic false positives (REST-JS request-payload reads), vendored libs/, or pre-existing CSS. Space-field hooks added to hooks_fired this session.
generated.refresh_2026_06_04: Pre-1.4.5 release refresh: coverage-gated hooks_fired reconcile - added 29 fired hooks missing since 1.4.3/1.4.4 (WB Gam filters, sidebar visibility/extension hooks, join-request + space-member events, delete post/reply); removed stale jetonomy_extensions_init (no longer in code). REST(66 routes incl /media via rest_base), tables(23), admin_pages(13 slugs), shortcodes(9) verified exact vs ground-truth grep. wp_cli(14)/blocks(8)/cron(6) registered dynamically (dispatcher loop / register_block_type / schedules array) - manifest counts verified correct. wppqa 2026-06-04 baseline identical to 2026-06-03 (all known FPs).
generated.delta_2026_06_12_ai_async: Moderation_Service: set_object_status() now rejects user_id <= 0 (anonymous can never moderate even if a permission_callback regresses); automated actors use the new system_set_object_status() (PHP-internal, never wire to REST/AJAX). Both share the private apply_object_status() impl, which also gained the hold=>pending action. Consumer: jetonomy-pro AI Batch_Reviewer.
generated.delta_2026_07_05_wave_e: Wave E: GDPR erase now covers jt_revisions (anonymize author_id), jt_spaces (anonymize author_id), jt_bookmarks (delete) in erase_data + on_user_delete (identity no longer survives an erase). NEW filter jetonomy_notification_deep_link (functions.php) so Pro object types (message/conversation) resolve to a URL without free hardcoding a pro route; notifications template + controller route through it. Admin fixes: idea_status_changed added to the notification_defaults save allowlist (toggle was dead); posts_per_page/replies_per_page clamped to 100 (was lower-bound only); option_page_capability_jetonomy_settings filter so a delegated jetonomy_manage_settings role can actually save. Follow-ups filed: GDPR export completeness + eraser batching + counter-recompute; reaction/flag_resolved notification types; dark_mode_default + verification_reminder_hours admin fields; notification-defaults render/consume default divergence.
generated.delta_2026_07_04_automator: Automator "our side" contract (card [Pro] Uncanny Automator integration): NEW SpaceMember::set_role() is the single role-update path (validates role vs SpaceMember::VALID_ROLES, not-a-member/idempotent guards, fires jetonomy_space_member_role_changed with old+new). REST update_member_role + admin ajax_change_member_role both route through it (killed the direct $wpdb->update dup + the silent add()-as-role-change). NEW filter jetonomy_before_set_role (veto). add()/remove() + join/leave events unchanged (still 3/2 args). VALID_ROLES centralized on the model. Automator SDK glue itself is out of scope (thin Pro/companion wrapper gated by class_exists(Automator)).
generated.delta_2026_07_04_reaction_flag_notifs: Wired 2 previously-consumerless events into notifications (free Notifier). NEW type reaction: on_first_reaction listens jetonomy_pro_first_reaction (Pro fires only on the FIRST reaction, 0->1, so the author gets one nudge not per-reaction spam) -> notify content author, skip self. NEW type flag_resolved: on_flag_resolved listens jetonomy_after_resolve_flag -> neutral acknowledgment to the reporter (no moderation outcome revealed), skip self-resolve. Both types registered in get_default_template + both admin allowlists (sanitize_email_templates + notif_types) + label maps + notification_defaults seed (web on, email off). Reuses create_and_maybe_email (private).
generated.delta_2026_07_04_custom_sitemap: NEW custom XML sitemap replacing the WP-core providers (which cannot emit <priority>/<changefreq>). Sitemap_Emitter (includes/seo/class-sitemap-emitter.php) serves /{base}-sitemap.xml (index) + /{base}-sitemap-{spaces|posts}-N.xml (children, 5000 URLs/page) via a router rewrite (jetonomy_route=sitemap) that echoes XML+exit. KEYSET pagination (id>cursor, cached cursors) — no OFFSET. New indexes sitemap_status_id (posts) + sitemap_vis_status_id (spaces) via migration 1_6_1 (JETONOMY_DB_VERSION->1.6.1). Transient cache (1h TTL). NEW filters jetonomy_sitemap_priority + jetonomy_sitemap_changefreq + action jetonomy_sitemap_prime_spaces (free defaults; Pro overrides). class-sitemap.php repurposed to add the robots.txt Sitemap line. DELETED class-spaces/posts-sitemap-provider.php. Router adds a redirect_canonical skip so /.xml is not 301->/.xml/.
generated.delta_2026_07_04_erase_batching: Privacy::erase_data() converted to WP page-batched eraser (bounded DELETE/UPDATE ... LIMIT per table per page via new jetonomy_erase_batch_size filter, default 1000; returns done=false until drained). The vote_score/member_count recompute now spans pages: affected ids captured on page 1 into a jetonomy_erase_<uid> transient, recomputed once on the final drained page. New filter jetonomy_erase_batch_size (hooks_fired).
generated.delta_2026_07_04_space_label: Configurable Space label: NEW jetonomy_space_label filter (functions.php space_label() helper) + settings keys space_label_singular/space_label_plural (Settings->General, sanitized in class-admin.php). Resolved label exposed to the mobile app via GET /app/config -> space_label{singular,plural}. Notification-defaults render table (settings.php) gains reaction + flag_resolved rows so they match the save whitelist (were force-disabled on every Email-tab save).
generated.refresh_2026_07_04: targeted delta after 1.6.0 self-audit round 2: added jetonomy_notification_deep_link + jetonomy_space_label filters (deterministic generator not used — clobbers registry-pattern REST/AJAX/CLI)
generated.delta_2026_07_17_support_round: 1.8.0 support round (9 commits, branch fix/1.8.0-support-round). Every item below was reproduced against a running site before it was written; several cards were refuted rather than implemented. ROUTING/SEO: NEW Router::suppress_default_query() (request filter) + Router::correct_query_state() (parse_query, prio 1) stop WP_Query falling through to page_for_posts on virtual routes. That fall-through was the root cause of Yoast publishing the Posts page's title AND a canonical pointing at it on every Space and topic. The fix is vendor-neutral by construction — there is no wpseo_* code in the tree; once the query stops lying, Yoast finds no indexable and defers, and Rank Math/AIOSEO/SEOPress/core are fixed by the same change. Router::maybe_serve_front_page() REMOVED; replaced by Router::is_mapped_front_page() + route derivation at template_redirect. TITLES: Schema_Markup::filter_title() and its document_title_parts filter REMOVED. It fought Template_Loader's closure — which registers later and therefore won — so every topic <title> rendered as the prettified SLUG. filter_title() also read get_option() raw and gated on !empty(), so the documented seo_post_title/seo_space_title defaults never applied and the feature silently no-opped on any install that had not explicitly saved a pattern. Pattern logic moved to Template_Loader::seo_title_from_pattern(), reading \Jetonomy\seo_settings() so the defaults apply. There is now exactly ONE document_title_parts producer. SPACES: NEW Space::visibility_levels() + Space::visibility_values() + \Jetonomy\space_visibility_options() — the single source for the visibility enum. It had been hand-written across 4 form surfaces and validated from 3 more hardcoded copies; two front-end forms had drifted and omitted `hidden`. A hidden space therefore rendered no selected option, the browser fell back to option[0] (public), and saving ANYTHING silently republished it to guests. Zero hand-written visibility lists remain on the space surface. Space::list_visible() now uses listing_visibility_sql() instead of its own inline gate — REST returned 26 spaces where the web rendered 32 for the same viewer, because list_visible() was never migrated when private became deliberately discoverable. The membership LEFT JOIN and its DISTINCT are gone with it, and jetonomy_space_listing_visibility_sql finally reaches REST. REPLIES: NEW \Jetonomy\reply_permalink() — THE single producer of a reply URL; all 7 hand-built '#reply-'.$id copies removed. NEW \Jetonomy\replies_per_page(); NEW Reply::pages_of() / prime_pages() / rows_by_id(); Reply::page_of() delegates to pages_of() so the ordering contract has one implementation rather than two that drift. reply_to_post notifications now store ('reply', reply_id). They previously stored ('post', post_id) and so could not name the reply at all — the customer-facing notification emitted no fragment whatsoever, which is why "take me to the reply" never worked. Old rows keep object_type=post and degrade to the bare topic URL; no migration, no 404. Reply cards render id="reply-N" (guests included — data-reply-id renders 0 times logged-out) plus a timestamp permalink, because there was previously NO way for anyone to share a link to a reply. :target handles scroll+highlight, so it survives with JS off (email, crawlers). ORDERING CONTRACT: page_of() computes under the default `oldest` sort and is sound ONLY because reply_permalink() emits no ?rsort — `newest` reverses the list and `best` orders by vote_score, which changes as people vote, so a page computed under it would rot. Documented in code with a journey test asserting the computation and the renderer still agree. PROFILE: jetonomy_profile_url is now honoured by the desktop AND mobile nav links, by @mentions, and by already-saved u/me/ menu items (resolved per-visitor via wp_setup_nav_menu_item, so existing menus are fixed without a migration). Mentions are batch-resolved — flat 3 queries regardless of mention count, vs 2N naive. Dead Mentions::parse() REMOVED; its user-existence validation was promoted into the live renderer, which had been linkifying any @word whether the user existed or not. CSS TOKENS: NEW --jt-accent-fg / --jt-accent-hover-fg, derived from the adopted accent via relative-colour oklch behind @supports. Hardcoded white on --jt-accent failed WCAG AA on 7 of 11 themes measured — including Reign, our own default, where the logged-out "Create free account" button sat at 2.13 (failing even the 3.0 large-text floor); it now measures 9.84. PHP cannot fix this: --jt-accent resolves to a theme token the browser only learns at runtime. The 0.57 threshold is derived, not chosen — sweeping 0.40-0.80 against 11 real theme accents, 0.56-0.58 is the only band where all 11 clear AA. Accent chain gained --nv-primary-accent (Neve, which exposes a brand token we were not reading) and now tails #0073aa to match the field default — it previously tailed #3B82F6, a value the UI never showed anywhere. PRO CONTRACT — verified, not assumed: Pro references NONE of the removed/renamed symbols (inherit_fonts, inherit_colors, Mentions::parse, filter_title, maybe_serve_front_page, --jtb-*). Pro touches \Jetonomy\notification_deep_link() at exactly 2 sites — private-messaging filters jetonomy_notification_deep_link for message/conversation types, and site-announcements passes an explicit 'post' type — both unaffected by the reply changes. Pro web-push consumes jetonomy_notification_created (7 args, unchanged) and now receives type=reply for reply notifications: that is the corrected base the plugin owes the app, and app-side handling is the app team's scope.

dead_listeners.note: Dead-fire-only — fired in moderation service after flag resolution, no listeners in either free or pro. Reserved as an extension point for custom listeners (Pro webhooks listens to the earlier jetonomy_flag_resolved hook instead).
